zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Metcalfe於1520年7月4日出生在英國英格蘭越可爾納帕時，她的父親Sir James Metcalfe60歲，母親Margaret Pigott27歲。她和William Hilton Esquire至少有8個兒子和10個女兒。她去世於1566年6月4日在英國英格蘭達勒姆諾斯 比迪可享年45歲，埋葬在英國達勒姆華盛頓。

nameMeanings.name: Metcalfe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Metcalf
meanings: English (Yorkshire): apparently a nickname from Middle English mete ‘meat, food’ + calf ‘calf’, i.e. ‘calf to be fattened up for eating’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Metcalfe was born on 4 July 1520, in Nappa, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir James Metcalfe, was 60 and her mother, Margaret Pigott, was 27. She had at least 8 sons and 10 daughters with William Hilton Esquire. She died on 4 June 1566, in North Biddick, Durham, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 45, and was buried in Washington, Durham, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Metcalfe est née le 4 juillet 1520, à Nappa, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir James Metcalfe, avait 60 ans et sa mère, Margaret Pigott, avait 27 ans. Elle a eu au moins 8 fils et 10 filles avec William Hilton Esquire. Elle est décédée le 4 juin 1566, à North Biddick, Durham, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterrée à Washington, Durham, Angleterre.

Juni 1566 im Alter von 45 Jahren in North Biddick, Durham, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Washington, Durham, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Metcalfeが1520年7月4日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアナッパで生まれた時，父Sir James Metcalfeは60歳，母Margaret Pigottは27歳であった。彼女はWilliam Hilton Esquireとの間に少なくとも8人の息子と10人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1566年6月4日にイギリスイギリスダルハムノース ビッドイックで45歳で死去し，イギリスダルハムワシュイントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Metcalfe(이)가 1520년 7월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 나파에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir James Metcalfe(은)는 60세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Pigott(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 William Hilton Esquire(와)과 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1566년 6월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람 노르스 비딕에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 뒤람 와신그턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Metcalfe родилась 4 июля 1520 г. в местности наппа, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir James Metcalfe было 60 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Pigott, 27. У неё было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 10 дочерей от William Hilton Esquire. Она умерла 4 июня 1566 г. в местности норт биддикк, дурам, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 45 лет и была похоронена в местности вашингтон, дурам, Англия.
